corrected_hover_or_pending_reason: pending: مِنِّى must show preposition مِن plus attached first-person pronoun and exact referent context; it cannot be certified through the تَمَنَّى verb candidate
drill_prompt: Segment مِنِّى and explain why it is not a تَمَنَّى wish-verb row.
learner_explanation: The ending on مِنِّى contributes me/Me, so a bare from string is not enough for rich certification.
nahw_lesson: Preposition plus pronoun rows need attachment and referent context before public wording.
sarf_lesson: Strict surface blocks a verb-entry candidate from certifying a preposition-plus-pronoun token.
what_failed: A preposition-plus-pronoun token entered a verb-entry tranche and looked populated because the English string was readable.

corrected_hover_or_pending_reason: pending: يَتَمَنَّوْهُ requires Form V imperfect plural host plus هُ object review before an authored hover such as they wish for it can be certified
drill_prompt: Explain why يَتَمَنَّوْهُ cannot be displayed as bare to desire or wish.
learner_explanation: The هُ at the end means it/him and must be visible in the hover explanation.
nahw_lesson: The object relation decides the English contribution, not the entry infinitive alone.
sarf_lesson: Finite derived verbs must preserve form, person/number, and suffix pronouns before hover trust.
visible_bad_hover: to desire or wish as a mental conception
what_failed: A finite verb token inherited entry prose and hid the attached object suffix.

corrected_hover_or_pending_reason: pending: كَادُوا needs kāda-sister construction review and the following predicate/context before the hover can choose almost/hardly wording
drill_prompt: For كَادُوا, identify what follows it and why generic to almost do is not a finished hover.
learner_explanation: كَادَ is a near-verb; its meaning depends on the following predicate and negation/context.
nahw_lesson: Kāda and sisters require construction-level review, not only a lexical gloss.
sarf_lesson: The token form alone does not decide whether the learner sees almost, nearly, or hardly in context.
visible_bad_hover: to almost do, but not do
what_failed: A near-verb construction was treated like a standalone dictionary verb.

corrected_hover_or_pending_reason: pending: مَوَازِينُهُۥ is a plural scales noun with هُ suffix; do not certify it from وَزَنَ verb evidence
drill_prompt: Segment مَوَازِينُهُۥ and explain why to weigh is the wrong kind of hover.
learner_explanation: The word names scales and carries a suffix meaning his/its; it is not the act of weighing.
nahw_lesson: The suffix referent and nominal role decide the visible wording.
sarf_lesson: A root family can contain nouns and verbs; the token POS and suffix decide the lane.
what_failed: A noun row in a verb-entry family inherited the finite/root verb gloss and hid the suffix.

corrected_hover_or_pending_reason: pending: مَّفْرُوضًا needs nominal/passive-participle or ordained-portion review; do not certify it from bare فَرَضَ verb prose
drill_prompt: Classify مَّفْرُوضًا by POS/derivative shape and explain why to ordain is not enough.
learner_explanation: The token describes an ordained portion, not merely the act to ordain.
nahw_lesson: Case and role decide whether the row is noun/adjective/participle wording in context.
sarf_lesson: Nominal derivatives inside verb-entry families need POS and pattern review before hover trust.
visible_bad_hover: to ordain, obligate, as if rules carved in stone
what_failed: A nominal derivative inherited entry verb prose instead of token contribution.
